354 | export function addDeliverableVersion(
355 |   id: string,
356 |   data: { version: string; note: string; fileUrl: string; uploadedBy: string }
357 | ): Deliverable | undefined {
358 |   const deliverable = deliverables.find((d) => d.id === id);
359 |   if (!deliverable) return undefined;
360 |   deliverable.versions.push({
361 |     version: data.version,
362 |     uploadedBy: data.uploadedBy,
363 |     uploadedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
364 |     note: data.note,
365 |     fileUrl: data.fileUrl,
366 |     agencyApprovalStatus: "pending",
367 |     clientApprovalStatus: "pending",

372 | export function approveVersionAgency(
373 |   deliverableId: string,
374 |   version: string,
375 |   approverId: string
376 | ): Deliverable | undefined {
377 |   const deliverable = deliverables.find((d) => d.id === deliverableId);
378 |   if (!deliverable) return undefined;
379 |   const ver = deliverable.versions.find((v) => v.version === version);
380 |   if (!ver) return undefined;
381 |   ver.agencyApprovalStatus = "approved";
382 |   ver.agencyApprovedBy = approverId;
383 |   ver.agencyApprovedAt = new Date().toISOString();
384 |   // If client already approved, this version becomes current
385 |   if (ver.clientApprovalStatus === "approved") {
386 |     deliverable.currentVersion = version;
387 |   }
388 |   return deliverable;
389 | }
390 |

393 |   version: string,
394 |   approverId: string
395 | ): Deliverable | undefined {
396 |   const deliverable = deliverables.find((d) => d.id === deliverableId);
397 |   if (!deliverable) return undefined;
398 |   const ver = deliverable.versions.find((v) => v.version === version);
399 |   if (!ver) return undefined;
400 |   ver.clientApprovalStatus = "approved";
401 |   ver.clientApprovedBy = approverId;
402 |   ver.clientApprovedAt = new Date().toISOString();
403 |   // If agency already approved, this version becomes current
404 |   if (ver.agencyApprovalStatus === "approved") {

# task_progress List (Optional - Plan Mode)

While in PLAN MODE, if you've outlined concrete steps or requirements for the user, you may include a preliminary todo list using the task_progress parameter.

Reminder on how to use the task_progress parameter:


1. To create or update a todo list, include the task_progress parameter in the next tool call
2. Review each item and update its status:
   - Mark completed items with: - [x]
   - Keep incomplete items as: - [ ]
   - Add new items if you discover additional steps
3. Modify the list as needed:
		- Add any new steps you've discovered
		- Reorder if the sequence has changed
4. Ensure the list accurately reflects the current state

**Remember:** Keeping the task_progress list updated helps track progress and ensures nothing is missed.

1 | """
2 | FastAPI application factory.
3 | 
4 | This module creates and configures the main FastAPI application with:
5 | - Database connection lifecycle management
6 | - API routers
7 | - Exception handlers
8 | - Health check endpoint
9 | """
10 | 
11 | import logging
12 | from contextlib import asynccontextmanager
13 | from typing import AsyncGenerator
14 | 
15 | from fastapi import FastAPI, HTTPException
16 | from fastapi.exception_handlers import http_exception_handler
17 | from fastapi.middleware.cors import CORSMiddleware
18 | from pydantic import BaseModel
19 | from app.api import api_router
20 | from app.db.database import check_database_connection, close_database_connection, SessionLocal
21 | from app.db.seed import seed_database
22 | 
23 | # Configure logging
24 | logging.basicConfig(
25 |     level=logging.INFO,
26 |     format="%(asctime)s - %(name)s - %(levelname)s - %(message)s",
27 | )
28 | 
29 | logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
30 | 
31 | 
32 | class HealthResponse(BaseModel):
33 |     """Response model for health check endpoint."""
34 |     
35 |     status: str
36 |     database: str
37 | 
38 | 
39 | @asynccontextmanager
40 | async def lifespan(app: FastAPI) -> AsyncGenerator[None, None]:
41 |     """
42 |     Application lifespan context manager.
43 |     
44 |     Handles startup and shutdown events:
45 |     - Startup: Verify database connection
46 |     - Shutdown: Close database connection pool
47 |     
48 |     Args:
49 |         app: The FastAPI application instance.
50 |         
51 |     Yields:
52 |         None
53 |     """
54 |     # Startup
55 |     logger.info("Starting up application...")
56 |     
57 |     # Check database connection
58 |     if check_database_connection():
59 |         logger.info("Database connection verified")
60 |         
61 |         # Seed database if tables are empty
62 |         with SessionLocal() as session:
63 |             seed_database(session)
64 |     else:
65 |         logger.warning("Database connection failed - some features may not work")
66 |     
67 |     yield
68 |     
69 |     # Shutdown
70 |     logger.info("Shutting down application...")
71 |     close_database_connection()
72 |     logger.info("Application shutdown complete")
73 | 
74 | 
75 | def create_app() -> FastAPI:
76 |     """
77 |     Create and configure the FastAPI application.
78 |     
79 |     Returns:
80 |         FastAPI: The configured FastAPI application instance.
81 |     """
82 |     app = FastAPI(
83 |         title="Creative Marketing Agency API",
84 |         description="""
85 |         Backend API for the Creative Marketing Agency Platform.
86 |         
87 |         This API provides endpoints for managing:
88 |         - Projects and milestones
89 |         - Tasks and timesheets
90 |         - Budgets and expenses
91 |         - Briefs and deliverables
92 |         - Users and clients
93 |         - Notifications and activity logs
94 |         """,
95 |         version="0.1.0",
96 |         docs_url="/docs",
97 |         redoc_url="/redoc",
98 |         openapi_url="/openapi.json",
99 |         lifespan=lifespan,
100 |     )
101 |     
102 |     # Add CORS middleware
103 |     # Configure for frontend origin to allow credentials (cookies)
104 |     app.add_middleware(
105 |         CORSMiddleware,
106 |         allow_origins=[
107 |             "http://localhost:3000",  # Frontend development server
108 |             # Add production frontend URL here when deployed
109 |         ],
110 |         allow_credentials=True,  # Required for cookies
111 |         allow_methods=["*"],
112 |         allow_headers=["*"],
113 |     )
114 |     
115 |     # Include API routers
116 |     app.include_router(api_router)
117 |     
118 |     # Health check endpoint
119 |     @app.get(
120 |         "/health",
121 |         response_model=HealthResponse,
122 |         tags=["health"],
123 |         summary="Health check",
124 |         description="Check the health status of the API and its dependencies.",
125 |     )
126 |     def health_check() -> HealthResponse:
127 |         """
128 |         Health check endpoint.
129 |         
130 |         Returns the health status of the API and database connection.
131 |         
132 |         Returns:
133 |             HealthResponse: Health status information.
134 |         """
135 |         db_status = "connected" if check_database_connection() else "disconnected"
136 |         return HealthResponse(
137 |             status="healthy",
138 |             database=db_status,
139 |         )
140 |     
141 |     # Root endpoint
142 |     @app.get(
143 |         "/",
144 |         tags=["root"],
145 |         summary="API root",
146 |         description="Returns basic information about the API.",
147 |     )
148 |     async def root() -> dict:
149 |         """
150 |         Root endpoint.
151 |         
152 |         Returns basic API information and links to documentation.
153 |         
154 |         Returns:
155 |             dict: API information.
156 |         """
157 |         return {
158 |             "name": "Creative Marketing Agency API",
159 |             "version": "0.1.0",
160 |             "docs": "/docs",
161 |             "redoc": "/redoc",
162 |         }
163 |     
164 |     # Custom exception handler for HTTP exceptions
165 |     @app.exception_handler(HTTPException)
166 |     async def custom_http_exception_handler(request, exc):
167 |         """
168 |         Custom HTTP exception handler.
169 |         
170 |         Logs the exception and returns the standard HTTP exception response.
171 |         """
172 |         logger.error(f"HTTP exception: {exc.status_code} - {exc.detail}")
173 |         return await http_exception_handler(request, exc)
174 |     
175 |     return app
176 | 
177 | 
178 | # Create the application instance
179 | app = create_app()

71 | @router.get(
72 |     "/{project_id}/deliverables",

74 |     summary="Get project deliverables",
75 |     description="Get all deliverables for a project with their versions, approval statuses, and resolved user names.",
76 | )
77 | def get_project_deliverables(
78 |     project_id: str,
79 |     current_user: Annotated[dict, Depends(get_current_user)],
80 |     db: Annotated[Session, Depends(get_db)],
81 | ) -> ProjectDeliverablesResponse:
82 |     role = current_user["role"]
83 |     user_id = current_user["user_id"]
84 | 
85 |     # =========================
86 |     # Project Exists
87 |     # =========================
88 |     project_row = db.execute(
89 |         text("SELECT id FROM projects WHERE id = :pid"),
90 |         {"pid": project_id},
91 |     ).fetchone()
92 | 
93 |     if not project_row:
94 |         raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Project not found")
95 | 
96 |     # =========================
97 |     # RBAC
98 |     # =========================
99 |     if role not in ("agency_owner", "ops_admin"):
100 |         has_access = False
101 | 
102 |         if role == "project_manager":
103 |             has_access = (
104 |                 db.execute(
105 |                     text(
106 |                         "SELECT 1 FROM projects WHERE id = :pid AND project_manager_id = :uid"
107 |                     ),
108 |                     {"pid": project_id, "uid": user_id},
109 |                 ).fetchone()
110 |                 is not None
111 |             )
112 | 
113 |         elif role == "team_member":
114 |             has_access = (
115 |                 db.execute(
116 |                     text(
117 |                         "SELECT 1 FROM project_team_members WHERE project_id = :pid AND user_id = :uid"
118 |                     ),
119 |                     {"pid": project_id, "uid": user_id},
120 |                 ).fetchone()
121 |                 is not None
122 |             )
123 | 
124 |         elif role == "client":
125 |             has_access = (
126 |                 db.execute(
127 |                     text("""
128 |                         SELECT 1 FROM projects p
129 |                         WHERE p.id = :pid
130 |                         AND p.client_id IN (SELECT id FROM clients WHERE user_id = :uid)
131 |                     """),
132 |                     {"pid": project_id, "uid": user_id},
133 |                 ).fetchone()
134 |                 is not None
135 |             )
136 | 
137 |         if not has_access:
138 |             raise HTTPException(
139 |                 status_code=403, detail="You don't have access to this project"
140 |             )
141 | 
142 |     # =========================
143 |     # Fetch Deliverables
144 |     # =========================
145 |     rows = db.execute(
146 |         text("""
147 |             SELECT
148 |                 d.id, d.project_id, d.milestone_id, d.task_id,
149 |                 d.title, d.description, d.assigned_to,
150 |                 COALESCE(au.name, 'Unassigned') AS assigned_to_name,
151 |                 d.status, d.current_version,
152 |                 d.approved_by, COALESCE(ap.name, '') AS approved_by_name,
153 |                 d.approved_at
154 |             FROM deliverables d
155 |             LEFT JOIN users au ON d.assigned_to = au.id
156 |             LEFT JOIN users ap ON d.approved_by = ap.id
157 |             WHERE d.project_id = :project_id
158 |             ORDER BY d.title ASC
159 |         """),
160 |         {"project_id": project_id},
161 |     ).fetchall()
162 | 
163 |     deliverable_ids = [r[0] for r in rows]
164 | 
165 |     # =========================
166 |     # Fetch Versions (batch)
167 |     # =========================
168 |     versions_by_dlv: dict[str, list[dict]] = {}
169 |     if deliverable_ids:
170 |         placeholders = ", ".join(f":id_{i}" for i in range(len(deliverable_ids)))
171 |         version_params = {f"id_{i}": did for i, did in enumerate(deliverable_ids)}
172 | 
173 |         version_rows = db.execute(
174 |             text(f"""
175 |                 SELECT
176 |                     dv.id, dv.deliverable_id, dv.version,
177 |                     dv.uploaded_by, COALESCE(uu.name, 'Unknown') AS uploaded_by_name,
178 |                     dv.uploaded_at, dv.note, dv.file_url,
179 |                     dv.agency_approval_status,
180 |                     dv.agency_approved_by, COALESCE(au.name, '') AS agency_approved_by_name,
181 |                     dv.agency_approved_at,
182 |                     dv.client_approval_status,
183 |                     dv.client_approved_by, COALESCE(cu.name, '') AS client_approved_by_name,
184 |                     dv.client_approved_at
185 |                 FROM deliverable_versions dv
186 |                 LEFT JOIN users uu ON dv.uploaded_by = uu.id
187 |                 LEFT JOIN users au ON dv.agency_approved_by = au.id
188 |                 LEFT JOIN users cu ON dv.client_approved_by = cu.id
189 |                 WHERE dv.deliverable_id IN ({placeholders})
190 |                 ORDER BY dv.uploaded_at ASC
191 |             """),
192 |             version_params,
193 |         ).fetchall()
194 | 
195 |         for vr in version_rows:
196 |             dlv_id = vr[1]
197 |             if dlv_id not in versions_by_dlv:
198 |                 versions_by_dlv[dlv_id] = []

204 |                     uploadedBy=vr[3],

213 |                     clientApprovalStatus=vr[12],

215 |                     clientApprovedByName=vr[14] or None,
216 |                     clientApprovedAt=vr[15].isoformat() if vr[15] else None,
217 |                 )
218 |             )
219 |

11 | from typing import Annotated
12 |

25 | # Schemas

27 |

29 | class CreateDeliverableRequest(BaseModel):
30 |     """Request body for creating a new deliverable."""
31 | 
32 |     title: str = Field(..., min_length=1, max_length=255, description="Deliverable title")
33 |     description: str = Field("", max_length=5000, description="Deliverable description")
34 |     milestoneId: str = Field(..., min_length=1, description="Milestone ID")
35 |     taskId: str = Field(..., min_length=1, description="Task ID")

42 |     projectId: str
43 |     milestoneId: str
44 |     taskId: str
45 |     title: str
46 |     description: str
47 |     assignedTo: str
48 |     assignedToName: str

59 |

63 |     return f"{prefix}_{uuid.uuid4().hex[:12]}"
64 | 
65 | 
66 | @router.post(
67 |     "/{project_id}/deliverables",
68 |     response_model=CreateDeliverableResponse,
69 |     status_code=201,
70 |     summary="Create a deliverable",
71 |     description="Create a new deliverable for a project. The assignee is derived from the task.",
72 | )
73 | def create_deliverable(
74 |     project_id: str,
75 |     body: CreateDeliverableRequest,
76 |     current_user: Annotated[dict, Depends(get_current_user)],
77 |     db: Annotated[Session, Depends(get_db)],
78 | ) -> CreateDeliverableResponse:
79 |     role = current_user["role"]
80 |     user_id = current_user["user_id"]
81 | 
82 |     # =========================
83 |     # RBAC — only agency_owner, ops_admin, project_manager
84 |     # =========================

86 |         raise HTTPException(

89 |         )

92 |     # Project Exists

94 |     project_row = db.execute(
95 |         text("SELECT id FROM projects WHERE id = :pid"),

97 |     ).fetchone()
98 | 
99 |     if not project_row:
100 |         raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Project not found")
101 | 
102 |     # Project manager can only create for their own projects
103 |     if role == "project_manager":
104 |         pm_check = db.execute(
105 |             text("SELECT 1 FROM projects WHERE id = :pid AND project_manager_id = :uid"),
106 |             {"pid": project_id, "uid": user_id},
107 |         ).fetchone()
108 |         if pm_check is None:
109 |             raise HTTPException(
110 |                 status_code=403,
111 |                 detail="You don't have access to this project",
112 |             )
113 | 
114 |     # =========================
115 |     # Validate Task belongs to project & get assigned_to
116 |     # =========================
117 |     task_row = db.execute(
118 |         text("""
119 |             SELECT t.id, t.assigned_to, t.milestone_id, COALESCE(u.name, 'Unassigned')
120 |             FROM tasks t
121 |             LEFT JOIN users u ON t.assigned_to = u.id
122 |             WHERE t.id = :task_id AND t.project_id = :project_id
123 |         """),
124 |         {"task_id": body.taskId, "project_id": project_id},
125 |     ).fetchone()
126 | 
127 |     if not task_row:
128 |         raise HTTPException(
129 |             status_code=404,
130 |             detail="Task not found in this project",
131 |         )

143 |         {"mid": body.milestoneId, "pid": project_id},
144 |     ).fetchone()
145 | 
146 |     if not milestone_row:
147 |         raise HTTPException(

150 |         )

154 |     # =========================

157 |

161 |             VALUES (:id, :project_id, :milestone_id, :task_id, :title, :description, :assigned_to, 'in_progress', '0')
162 |         """),

169 |             "description": body.description.strip(),

171 |         },
172 |     )

175 |     # Create Initial Version (1.0)
176 |     # =========================
177 |     version_id = _generate_id("dv")
178 | 
179 |     db.execute(
180 |         text("""
181 |             INSERT INTO deliverable_versions
182 |                 (id, deliverable_id, version, uploaded_by, uploaded_at, note, file_url,
183 |                  agency_approval_status, client_approval_status)
184 |             VALUES
185 |                 (:id, :deliverable_id, '1.0', :uploaded_by, :uploaded_at, 'Initial submission', '',
186 |                  'pending', 'pending')
187 |         """),
188 |         {
189 |             "id": version_id,
190 |             "deliverable_id": deliverable_id,
191 |             "uploaded_by": assigned_to,
192 |             "uploaded_at": now,
193 |         },
194 |     )
195 | 
196 |     db.commit()
197 | 
198 |     return CreateDeliverableResponse(
199 |         id=deliverable_id,
200 |         projectId=project_id,
201 |         milestoneId=body.milestoneId,
202 |         taskId=task_id,
203 |         title=body.title.strip(),
204 |         description=body.description.strip(),
205 |         assignedTo=assigned_to,
206 |         assignedToName=assigned_to_name,
207 |         status="in_progress",
208 |         currentVersion="0",
209 |         approvedBy=None,
210 |         approvedByName=None,
211 |         approvedAt=None,
212 |     )

(File has 212 lines total.)

# task_progress List (Optional - Plan Mode)

While in PLAN MODE, if you've outlined concrete steps or requirements for the user, you may include a preliminary todo list using the task_progress parameter.

Reminder on how to use the task_progress parameter:


1. To create or update a todo list, include the task_progress parameter in the next tool call
2. Review each item and update its status:
   - Mark completed items with: - [x]
   - Keep incomplete items as: - [ ]
   - Add new items if you discover additional steps
3. Modify the list as needed:
		- Add any new steps you've discovered
		- Reorder if the sequence has changed
4. Ensure the list accurately reflects the current state

**Remember:** Keeping the task_progress list updated helps track progress and ensures nothing is missed.

While in PLAN MODE, if you've outlined concrete steps or requirements for the user, you may include a preliminary todo list using the task_progress parameter.

Reminder on how to use the task_progress parameter:


1. To create or update a todo list, include the task_progress parameter in the next tool call

   - Mark completed items with: - [x]
   - Keep incomplete items as: - [ ]
   - Add new items if you discover additional steps
3. Modify the list as needed:
		- Add any new steps you've discovered
		- Reorder if the sequence has changed
4. Ensure the list accurately reflects the current state

**Remember:** Keeping the task_progress list updated helps track progress and ensures nothing is missed.

## Plan: Replace Static/Mock Data with Real APIs for Deliverable Detail Page

### Current State Analysis

The deliverable detail page (`frontend/components/projects/deliverable-detail-page.tsx`) uses 6 static data modules:
- `@/data/projects` → `getProjectById`
- `@/data/deliverables` → `getDeliverableById`, `addDeliverableVersion`, `approveVersionAgency`, `approveVersionClient`
- `@/data/milestones` → `getMilestoneById`
- `@/data/tasks` → `getTaskById`
- `@/data/users` → `getUserById`
- `@/data/comments` → `getCommentsByVersion`, `addComment`

The backend already has:
- `GET /api/projects/{projectId}/deliverables` (list all)
- `POST /api/projects/{projectId}/deliverables` (create)

The `backend/app/api/projects/deliverables/` subfolder exists but is empty.

---

### Backend: New API Endpoints

Create files in `backend/app/api/projects/deliverables/`:

#### 1. `__init__.py` — Router aggregation
```python
from fastapi import APIRouter
from .detail import router as detail_router
from .versions import router as versions_router
from .approvals import router as approvals_router
from .comments import router as comments_router

router = APIRouter()
router.include_router(detail_router)
router.include_router(versions_router)
router.include_router(approvals_router)
router.include_router(comments_router)
```

#### 2. `detail.py` — `GET /{project_id}/deliverables/{deliverable_id}`
Returns single deliverable with all versions, resolved user names, milestone title, task title. Schemas:
- `DeliverableDetailResponse` — full deliverable with versions, milestone info, task info, assignee info

#### 3. `versions.py` — `POST /{project_id}/deliverables/{deliverable_id}/versions`
Upload a new version. Request body: `version`, `note`, `fileUrl`. Server sets `uploadedBy` from current user, `uploadedAt` to now. Resets both approval statuses to `pending`.

#### 4. `approvals.py` — Two endpoints:
- `POST /{project_id}/deliverables/{deliverable_id}/versions/{version}/approve-agency`
- `POST /{project_id}/deliverables/{deliverable_id}/versions/{version}/approve-client`

Sets the respective approval status to `approved`, records approver and timestamp. If both approvals are now `approved`, updates `deliverable.current_version` and sets status to `approved`.

#### 5. `comments.py` — Two endpoints:
- `GET /{project_id}/deliverables/{deliverable_id}/versions/{version}/comments`
  Returns threaded comments with resolved user names.
- `POST /{project_id}/deliverables/{deliverable_id}/versions/{version}/comments`
  Creates a comment. Request body: `content`, `parentId` (optional). Server sets `userId`, `createdAt`.

#### 6. Update `backend/app/api/projects/__init__.py`
Add import and include for the new deliverables sub-router:
```python
from app.api.projects.deliverables import router as deliverables_detail_router
router.include_router(deliverables_detail_router)
```

---

### Frontend: Server Actions

Create `frontend/actions/deliverables.ts` with:

- `getDeliverableDetailAction(projectId, deliverableId)` → calls `GET .../deliverables/{deliverableId}`
- `uploadDeliverableVersionAction(projectId, deliverableId, data)` → calls `POST .../versions`
- `approveAgencyAction(projectId, deliverableId, version)` → calls `POST .../approve-agency`
- `approveClientAction(projectId, deliverableId, version)` → calls `POST .../approve-client`
- `getDeliverableCommentsAction(projectId, deliverableId, version)` → calls `GET .../comments`
- `addDeliverableCommentAction(projectId, deliverableId, version, data)` → calls `POST .../comments`

---

### Frontend: Component Updates

Update `frontend/components/projects/deliverable-detail-page.tsx`:

1. **Define local types** (no imports from `@/data/*`):
   - `DeliverableStatus`, `VersionApprovalStatus`, `DeliverableVersion`, `Deliverable` (from deliverables data)
   - `Comment` (from comments data)

2. **Replace static function calls with API calls:**
   - `getProjectById(projectId)` → fetch project detail (use existing `getProjectOverviewStatsAction` or a lightweight project fetch)
   - `getDeliverableById(deliverableId)` → `getDeliverableDetailAction(projectId, deliverableId)`
   - `getMilestoneById(milestoneId)` → use milestone data from the deliverable detail response (include milestone title)
   - `getTaskById(taskId)` → use task data from the deliverable detail response (include task title)
   - `getUserById(userId)` → use resolved names from API response (e.g., `assignedToName`, `uploadedByName`, etc.)
   - `getCommentsByVersion(deliverableId, version)` → `getDeliverableCommentsAction(projectId, deliverableId, version)`
   - `addComment(...)` → `addDeliverableCommentAction(projectId, deliverableId, version, data)`
   - `addDeliverableVersion(...)` → `uploadDeliverableVersionAction(projectId, deliverableId, data)`
   - `approveVersionAgency(...)` → `approveAgencyAction(projectId, deliverableId, version)`
   - `approveVersionClient(...)` → `approveClientAction(projectId, deliverableId, version)`

3. **Convert to async data fetching:**
   - Use `useState` + `useEffect` to fetch deliverable detail on mount
   - Use `useState` + `useEffect` in `VersionComments` to fetch comments
   - After mutations (upload, approve, comment), refetch relevant data
   - Remove the `forceUpdate` hack — use proper state management

4. **Remove all imports from `@/data/*`:**
   - `@/data/projects`
   - `@/data/deliverables`
   - `@/data/milestones`
   - `@/data/tasks`
   - `@/data/users`
   - `@/data/comments`

---

### Files to Create/Modify

| Action | File |
|--------|------|
| CREATE | `backend/app/api/projects/deliverables/__init__.py` |
| CREATE | `backend/app/api/projects/deliverables/detail.py` |
| CREATE | `backend/app/api/projects/deliverables/versions.py` |
| CREATE | `backend/app/api/projects/deliverables/approvals.py` |
| CREATE | `backend/app/api/projects/deliverables/comments.py` |
| MODIFY | `backend/app/api/projects/__init__.py` |
| CREATE | `frontend/actions/deliverables.ts` |
| MODIFY | `frontend/components/projects/deliverable-detail-page.tsx` |

---

### Implementation Order

1. Backend deliverable detail endpoint (the core GET)
2. Backend versions endpoint (upload)
3. Backend approvals endpoints (agency + client)
4. Backend comments endpoints (get + create)
5. Update backend router registration
6. Frontend server actions
7. Frontend component refactor

<write_to_file>
<path>backend/app/api/projects/deliverables/detail.py</path>
<content>"""
Deliverable Detail API endpoint.

GET /api/projects/{project_id}/deliverables/{deliverable_id}
Returns a single deliverable with all its versions, resolved user names,
milestone title, and task title.
"""

from typing import Annotated, Optional

from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends, HTTPException
from pydantic import BaseModel
from sqlalchemy import text
from sqlalchemy.orm import Session

from app.auth.dependencies import get_current_user
from app.db.dependencies import get_db

router = APIRouter()


# =========================
# Schemas
# =========================


class DeliverableVersionItem(BaseModel):
    id: str
    deliverableId: str
    version: str
    uploadedBy: str
    uploadedByName: str
    uploadedAt: str
    note: str
    fileUrl: str
    agencyApprovalStatus: str
    agencyApprovedBy: Optional[str] = None
    agencyApprovedByName: Optional[str] = None
    agencyApprovedAt: Optional[str] = None
    clientApprovalStatus: str
    clientApprovedBy: Optional[str] = None
    clientApprovedByName: Optional[str] = None
    clientApprovedAt: Optional[str] = None


class DeliverableDetailResponse(BaseModel):
    id: str
    projectId: str
    milestoneId: str
    milestoneTitle: str
    taskId: str
    taskTitle: str
    title: str
    description: str
    assignedTo: str
    assignedToName: str
    status: str
    currentVersion: str
    approvedBy: Optional[str] = None
    approvedByName: Optional[str] = None
    approvedAt: Optional[str] = None
    versions: list[DeliverableVersionItem]


# =========================
# Endpoint
# =========================


@router.get(
    "/{project_id}/deliverables/{deliverable_id}",
    response_model=DeliverableDetailResponse,
    summary="Get deliverable detail",
    description="Get a single deliverable with all versions, resolved names, milestone and task info.",
)
def get_deliverable_detail(
    project_id: str,
    deliverable_id: str,
    current_user: Annotated[dict, Depends(get_current_user)],
    db: Annotated[Session, Depends(get_db)],
) -> DeliverableDetailResponse:
    role = current_user["role"]
    user_id = current_user["user_id"]

    # =========================
    # RBAC
    # =========================
    if role not in ("agency_owner", "ops_admin"):
        has_access = False

        if role == "project_manager":
            has_access = (
                db.execute(
                    text(
                        "SELECT 1 FROM projects WHERE id = :pid AND project_manager_id = :uid"
                    ),
                    {"pid": project_id, "uid": user_id},
                ).fetchone()
                is not None
            )

        elif role == "team_member":
            has_access = (
                db.execute(
                    text(
                        "SELECT 1 FROM project_team_members WHERE project_id = :pid AND user_id = :uid"
                    ),
                    {"pid": project_id, "uid": user_id},
                ).fetchone()
                is not None
            )

        elif role == "client":
            has_access = (
                db.execute(
                    text("""
                        SELECT 1 FROM projects p
                        WHERE p.id = :pid

"""
Deliverable Detail API endpoint.

GET /api/projects/{project_id}/deliverables/{deliverable_id}
Returns a single deliverable with all its versions, resolved user names,
milestone title, and task title.
"""

from typing import Annotated, Optional

from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends, HTTPException
from pydantic import BaseModel
from sqlalchemy import text
from sqlalchemy.orm import Session

from app.auth.dependencies import get_current_user
from app.db.dependencies import get_db

router = APIRouter()


# =========================
# Schemas
# =========================


class DeliverableVersionItem(BaseModel):
    id: str
    deliverableId: str
    version: str
    uploadedBy: str
    uploadedByName: str
    uploadedAt: str
    note: str
    fileUrl: str
    agencyApprovalStatus: str
    agencyApprovedBy: Optional[str] = None
    agencyApprovedByName: Optional[str] = None
    agencyApprovedAt: Optional[str] = None
    clientApprovalStatus: str
    clientApprovedBy: Optional[str] = None
    clientApprovedByName: Optional[str] = None
    clientApprovedAt: Optional[str] = None


class DeliverableDetailResponse(BaseModel):
    id: str
    projectId: str
    milestoneId: str
    milestoneTitle: str
    taskId: str
    taskTitle: str
    title: str
    description: str
    assignedTo: str
    assignedToName: str
    status: str
    currentVersion: str
    approvedBy: Optional[str] = None
    approvedByName: Optional[str] = None
    approvedAt: Optional[str] = None
    versions: list[DeliverableVersionItem]


# =========================
# Endpoint
# =========================


@router.get(
    "/{project_id}/deliverables/{deliverable_id}",
    response_model=DeliverableDetailResponse,
    summary="Get deliverable detail",
    description="Get a single deliverable with all versions, resolved names, milestone and task info.",
)
def get_deliverable_detail(
    project_id: str,
    deliverable_id: str,
    current_user: Annotated[dict, Depends(get_current_user)],
    db: Annotated[Session, Depends(get_db)],
) -> DeliverableDetailResponse:
    role = current_user["role"]
    user_id = current_user["user_id"]

    # =========================
    # RBAC
    # =========================
    if role not in ("agency_owner", "ops_admin"):
        has_access = False

        if role == "project_manager":
            has_access = (
                db.execute(
                    text(
                        "SELECT 1 FROM projects WHERE id = :pid AND project_manager_id = :uid"
                    ),
                    {"pid": project_id, "uid": user_id},
                ).fetchone()
                is not None
            )

        elif role == "team_member":
            has_access = (
                db.execute(
                    text(
                        "SELECT 1 FROM project_team_members WHERE project_id = :pid AND user_id = :uid"
                    ),
                    {"pid": project_id, "uid": user_id},
                ).fetchone()
                is not None
            )

        elif role == "client":
            has_access = (
                db.execute(
                    text("""
                        SELECT 1 FROM projects p
                        WHERE p.id = :pid
                        AND p.client_id IN (SELECT id FROM clients WHERE user_id = :uid)
                    """),
                    {"pid": project_id, "uid": user_id},
                ).fetchone()
                is not None
            )

        if not has_access:
            raise HTTPException(
                status_code=403, detail="You don't have access to this project"
            )

    # =========================
    # Fetch Deliverable
    # =========================
    row = db.execute(
        text("""
            SELECT
                d.id, d.project_id, d.milestone_id, d.task_id,
                d.title, d.description, d.assigned_to,
                COALESCE(au.name, 'Unassigned') AS assigned_to_name,
                d.status, d.current_version,
                d.approved_by, COALESCE(ap.name, '') AS approved_by_name,
                d.approved_at,
                COALESCE(m.title, '') AS milestone_title,
                COALESCE(t.title, '') AS task_title
            FROM deliverables d
            LEFT JOIN users au ON d.assigned_to = au.id
            LEFT JOIN users ap ON d.approved_by = ap.id
            LEFT JOIN milestones m ON d.milestone_id = m.id
            LEFT JOIN tasks t ON d.task_id = t.id
            WHERE d.id = :deliverable_id AND d.project_id = :project_id
        """),
        {"deliverable_id": deliverable_id, "project_id": project_id},
    ).fetchone()

    if not row:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Deliverable not found")

    # =========================
    # Fetch Versions
    # =========================
    version_rows = db.execute(
        text("""
            SELECT
                dv.id, dv.deliverable_id, dv.version,
                dv.uploaded_by, COALESCE(uu.name, 'Unknown') AS uploaded_by_name,
                dv.uploaded_at, dv.note, dv.file_url,
                dv.agency_approval_status,
                dv.agency_approved_by, COALESCE(au.name, '') AS agency_approved_by_name,
                dv.agency_approved_at,
                dv.client_approval_status,
                dv.client_approved_by, COALESCE(cu.name, '') AS client_approved_by_name,
                dv.client_approved_at
            FROM deliverable_versions dv
            LEFT JOIN users uu ON dv.uploaded_by = uu.id
            LEFT JOIN users au ON dv.agency_approved_by = au.id
            LEFT JOIN users cu ON dv.client_approved_by = cu.id
            WHERE dv.deliverable_id = :deliverable_id
            ORDER BY dv.uploaded_at ASC
        """),
        {"deliverable_id": deliverable_id},
    ).fetchall()

    versions = [
        DeliverableVersionItem(
            id=vr[0],
            deliverableId=vr[1],
            version=vr[2],
            uploadedBy=vr[3],
            uploadedByName=vr[4],
            uploadedAt=vr[5].isoformat() if vr[5] else "",
            note=vr[6],
            fileUrl=vr[7],
            agencyApprovalStatus=vr[8],

        )
        for vr in version_rows
    ]

    return DeliverableDetailResponse(

        title=row[4],
        description=row[5],

<content>"""
Deliverable Versions API endpoint.

POST /api/projects/{project_id}/deliverables/{deliverable_id}/versions
Upload a new version for a deliverable.
"""

import uuid
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from typing import Annotated

from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends, HTTPException
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
from sqlalchemy import text
from sqlalchemy.orm import Session

from app.auth.dependencies import get_current_user
from app.db.dependencies import get_db

router = APIRouter()


# =========================
# Schemas
# =========================


class UploadVersionRequest(BaseModel):
    """Request body for uploading a new deliverable version."""

    version: str = Field(..., min_length=1, max_length=10, description="Version string e.g. '2.0'")
    note: str = Field("", max_length=5000, description="Description of what changed")
    fileUrl: str = Field("", max_length=500, description="URL to the uploaded file")


class UploadVersionResponse(BaseModel):
    """Response after uploading a version."""

    id: str
    deliverableId: str
    version: str
    uploadedBy: str
    uploadedByName: str
    uploadedAt: str
    note: str
    fileUrl: str
    agencyApprovalStatus: str
    clientApprovalStatus: str


# =========================
# Helpers
# =========================


def _generate_id(prefix: str) -> str:
    """Generate a unique ID with the given prefix."""
    return f"{prefix}_{uuid.uuid4().hex[:12]}"


# =========================
# Endpoint
# =========================


@router.post(
    "/{project_id}/deliverables/{deliverable_id}/versions",
    response_model=UploadVersionResponse,
    status_code=201,
    summary="Upload deliverable version",
    description="Upload a new version for a deliverable. Resets approval statuses to pending.",
)
def upload_deliverable_version(
    project_id: str,
    deliverable_id: str,
    body: UploadVersionRequest,
    current_user: Annotated[dict, Depends(get_current_user)],
    db: Annotated[Session, Depends(get_db)],
) -> UploadVersionResponse:
    role = current_user["role"]
    user_id = current_user["user_id"]

    # =========================
    # RBAC — agency side or assigned user
    # =========================
    if role not in ("agency_owner", "ops_admin", "project_manager", "team_member"):
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=403,
            detail="You don't have permission to upload versions",
        )

    # =========================
    # Verify deliverable exists in this project
    # =========================
    dlv_row = db.execute(
        text("""
            SELECT d.assigned_to, COALESCE(u.name, 'Unknown')
            FROM deliverables d
            LEFT JOIN users u ON d.assigned_to = u.id
            WHERE d.id = :did AND d.project_id = :pid
        """),
        {"did": deliverable_id, "pid": project_id},
    ).fetchone()

    if not dlv_row:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Deliverable not found")

    assigned_to = dlv_row[0]

    # Only the assigned user or agency staff can upload
    if role == "team_member" and user_id != assigned_to:
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=403,
            detail="Only the assigned team member can upload versions",
        )

    # =========================
    # Check version doesn't already exist
    # =========================
    existing = db.execute(
        text(
            "SELECT 1 FROM deliverable_versions WHERE deliverable_id = :did AND version = :ver"
        ),
        {"did": deliverable_id, "ver": body.version.strip()},
    ).fetchone()

    if existing:
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=409,
            detail=f"Version {body.version.strip()} already exists for this deliverable",
        )

    # =========================
    # Create Version
    # =========================
    version_id = _generate_id("dv")
    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)

    db.execute(
        text("""
            INSERT INTO deliverable_versions
                (id, deliverable_id, version, uploaded_by, uploaded_at, note, file_url,
                 agency_approval_status, client_approval_status)
            VALUES
                (:id, :did, :version, :uploaded_by, :uploaded_at, :note, :file_url,
                 'pending', 'pending')
        """),
        {
            "id": version_id,
            "did": deliverable_id,
            "version": body.version.strip(),
            "uploaded_by": user_id,
            "uploaded_at": now,
            "note": body.note.strip(),
            "file_url": body.fileUrl.strip(),
        },
    )

    # =========================
    # Update deliverable status to in_review
    # =========================
    db.execute(
        text("""
            UPDATE deliverables
            SET status = 'in_review'
            WHERE id = :did
        """),
        {"did": deliverable_id},
    )

    db.commit()

    uploader_name = db.execute(
        text("SELECT COALESCE(name, 'Unknown') FROM users WHERE id = :uid"),
        {"uid": user_id},
    ).fetchone()

    return UploadVersionResponse(
        id=version_id,
        deliverableId=deliverable_id,
        version=body.version.strip(),
        uploadedBy=user_id,
        uploadedByName=uploader_name[0] if uploader_name else "Unknown",
        uploadedAt=now.isoformat(),
        note=body.note.strip(),
        fileUrl=body.fileUrl.strip(),
        agencyApprovalStatus="pending",
        clientApprovalStatus="pending",
    )
</content>

<content>"""
Deliverable Approvals API endpoints.

POST /api/projects/{project_id}/deliverables/{deliverable_id}/versions/{version}/approve-agency
POST /api/projects/{project_id}/deliverables/{deliverable_id}/versions/{version}/approve-client
"""

from datetime import datetime, timezone
from typing import Annotated

from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends, HTTPException
from pydantic import BaseModel
from sqlalchemy import text
from sqlalchemy.orm import Session

from app.auth.dependencies import get_current_user
from app.db.dependencies import get_db

router = APIRouter()


# =========================
# Schemas
# =========================


class ApprovalResponse(BaseModel):
    """Response after an approval action."""

    deliverableId: str
    version: str
    agencyApprovalStatus: str

) -> ApprovalResponse:

        )

    dlv_row = db.execute(

    if not dlv_row:

    ver_row = db.execute(
        text("""

            FROM deliverable_versions
            WHERE deliverable_id = :did AND version = :ver
        """),
        {"did": deliverable_id, "ver": version},
    ).fetchone()

    if not ver_row:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Version not found")

    if ver_row[0] == "approved":
        raise HTTPException(status_code=409, detail="Agency already approved this version")

    # =========================

    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)

        text("""

            SET agency_approval_status = 'approved',
                agency_approved_by = :uid,
                agency_approved_at = :now
            WHERE deliverable_id = :did AND version = :ver
        """),
        {"uid": user_id, "now": now, "did": deliverable_id, "ver": version},
    )

    # Check if client already approved — if so, make this version current
    client_status = ver_row[1]
    new_current_version = ""
    new_status = "in_review"

    if client_status == "approved":
        db.execute(

                SET current_version = :ver, status = 'approved'

<path>backend/app/api/projects/deliverables/comments.py</path>
<content>"""
Deliverable Version Comments API endpoints.

GET  /api/projects/{project_id}/deliverables/{deliverable_id}/versions/{version}/comments
POST /api/projects/{project_id}/deliverables/{deliverable_id}/versions/{version}/comments
"""

import uuid
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from typing import Annotated, Optional

from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends, HTTPException
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
from sqlalchemy import text
from sqlalchemy.orm import Session

from app.auth.dependencies import get_current_user
from app.db.dependencies import get_db

router = APIRouter()


# =========================
# Schemas
# =========================


class CommentItem(BaseModel):
    id: str
    deliverableId: str
    deliverableVersion: str
    userId: str
    userName: str
    content: str
    createdAt: str
    parentId: Optional[str] = None


class GetCommentsResponse(BaseModel):
    comments: list[CommentItem]


class AddCommentRequest(BaseModel):
    """Request body for adding a comment."""

    content: str = Field(..., min_length=1, max_length=5000, description="Comment content")
    parentId: Optional[str] = Field(None, description="Parent comment ID for replies")


class AddCommentResponse(BaseModel):
    """Response after adding a comment."""

    id: str
    deliverableId: str
    deliverableVersion: str
    userId: str
    userName: str
    content: str
    createdAt: str
    parentId: Optional[str] = None


# =========================
# Helpers
# =========================


def _generate_id(prefix: str) -> str:
    """Generate a unique ID with the given prefix."""
    return f"{prefix}_{uuid.uuid4().hex[:12]}"


# =========================
# RBAC Helper
# =========================


def _check_access(
    project_id: str,
    deliverable_id: str,
    user_id: str,
    role: str,
    db: Session,
) -> None:
    """Check user has access to this deliverable. Raises HTTPException if not."""
    # First verify deliverable exists in project
    dlv_row = db.execute(
        text("SELECT 1 FROM deliverables WHERE id = :did AND project_id = :pid"),
        {"did": deliverable_id, "pid": project_id},
    ).fetchone()

    if not dlv_row:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Deliverable not found")

    if role in ("agency_owner", "ops_admin"):
        return

    has_access = False

    if role == "project_manager":
        has_access = (
            db.execute(
                text(
                    "SELECT 1 FROM projects WHERE id = :pid AND project_manager_id = :uid"
                ),
                {"pid": project_id, "uid": user_id},
            ).fetchone()
            is not None
        )

    elif role == "team_member":
        has_access = (
            db.execute(
                text(
                    "SELECT 1 FROM project_team_members WHERE project_id = :pid AND user_id = :uid"
                ),
                {"pid": project_id, "uid": user_id},
            ).fetchone()
            is not None
        )

    elif role == "client":
        has_access = (
            db.execute(
                text("""
                    SELECT 1 FROM projects p
                    WHERE p.id = :pid
                    AND p.client_id IN (SELECT id FROM clients WHERE user_id = :uid)
                """),
                {"pid": project_id, "uid": user_id},
            ).fetchone()
            is not None
        )

    if not has_access:
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=403, detail="You don't have access to this project"
        )


# =========================
# Endpoint: Get Comments
# =========================


@router.get(
    "/{project_id}/deliverables/{deliverable_id}/versions/{version}/comments",
    response_model=GetCommentsResponse,
    summary="Get version comments",
    description="Get all comments for a specific deliverable version, ordered by creation time.",
)
def get_version_comments(
    project_id: str,
    deliverable_id: str,
    version: str,
    current_user: Annotated[dict, Depends(get_current_user)],
    db: Annotated[Session, Depends(get_db)],
) -> GetCommentsResponse:
    role = current_user["role"]
    user_id = current_user["user_id"]

    _check_access(project_id, deliverable_id, user_id, role, db)

    rows = db.execute(
        text("""
            SELECT
                c.id, c.deliverable_id, c.deliverable_version,
                c.user_id, COALESCE(u.name, 'Unknown') AS user_name,
                c.content, c.created_at, c.parent_id
            FROM comments c
            LEFT JOIN users u ON c.user_id = u.id
            WHERE c.deliverable_id = :did AND c.deliverable_version = :ver
            ORDER BY c.created_at ASC
        """),
        {"did": deliverable_id, "ver": version},
    ).fetchall()

    comments = [
        CommentItem(
            id=r[0],
            deliverableId=r[1],
            deliverableVersion=r[2],
            userId=r[3],
            userName=r[4],
            content=r[5],
            createdAt=r[6].isoformat() if r[6] else "",
            parentId=r[7] or None,
        )
        for r in rows
    ]

    return GetCommentsResponse(comments=comments)


# =========================
# Endpoint: Add Comment
# =========================


@router.post(
    "/{project_id}/deliverables/{deliverable_id}/versions/{version}/comments",
    response_model=AddCommentResponse,
    status_code=201,
    summary="Add version comment",
    description="Add a comment to a specific deliverable version. Supports threaded replies via parentId.",
)
def add_version_comment(
    project_id: str,
    deliverable_id: str,
    version: str,
    body: AddCommentRequest,
    current_user: Annotated[dict, Depends(get_current_user)],
    db: Annotated[Session, Depends(get_db)],
) -> AddCommentResponse:
    role = current_user["role"]
    user_id = current_user["user_id"]

    _check_access(project_id, deliverable_id, user_id, role, db)

    # Validate parentId if provided
    if body.parentId:
        parent = db.execute(
            text("SELECT 1 FROM comments WHERE id = :pid"),
            {"pid": body.parentId},
        ).fetchone()
        if not parent:
            raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Parent comment not found")

    # =========================
    # Create Comment
    # =========================
    comment_id = _generate_id("cmt")
    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)

    db.execute(
        text("""
            INSERT INTO comments
                (id, deliverable_id, deliverable_version, user_id, content, created_at, parent_id)
            VALUES
                (:id, :did, :ver, :uid, :content, :created_at, :parent_id)
        """),
        {
            "id": comment_id,
            "did": deliverable_id,
            "ver": version,
            "uid": user_id,
            "content": body.content.strip(),
            "created_at": now,
            "parent_id": body.parentId,
        },
    )

    db.commit()

    user_name = db.execute(
        text("SELECT COALESCE(name, 'Unknown') FROM users WHERE id = :uid"),
        {"uid": user_id},
    ).fetchone()

    return AddCommentResponse(
        id=comment_id,
        deliverableId=deliverable_id,
        deliverableVersion=version,
        userId=user_id,
        userName=user_name[0] if user_name else "Unknown",
        content=body.content.strip(),
        createdAt=now.isoformat(),
        parentId=body.parentId,
    )

# =========================


def _generate_id(prefix: str) -> str:
    """Generate a unique ID with the given prefix."""
    return f"{prefix}_{uuid.uuid4().hex[:12]}"


# =========================
# RBAC Helper
# =========================


def _check_access(
    project_id: str,
    deliverable_id: str,
    user_id: str,
    role: str,
    db: Session,
) -> None:
    """Check user has access to this deliverable. Raises HTTPException if not."""
    # First verify deliverable exists in project
    dlv_row = db.execute(
        text("SELECT 1 FROM deliverables WHERE id = :did AND project_id = :pid"),
        {"did": deliverable_id, "pid": project_id},
    ).fetchone()

    if not dlv_row:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Deliverable not found")

    if role in ("agency_owner", "ops_admin"):
        return

    has_access = False

    if role == "project_manager":
        has_access = (
            db.execute(
                text(
                    "SELECT 1 FROM projects WHERE id = :pid AND project_manager_id = :uid"
                ),
                {"pid": project_id, "uid": user_id},
            ).fetchone()
            is not None
        )

    elif role == "team_member":
        has_access = (
            db.execute(
                text(
                    "SELECT 1 FROM project_team_members WHERE project_id = :pid AND user_id = :uid"
                ),
                {"pid": project_id, "uid": user_id},
            ).fetchone()
            is not None
        )

    elif role == "client":
        has_access = (
            db.execute(
                text("""
                    SELECT 1 FROM projects p
                    WHERE p.id = :pid
                    AND p.client_id IN (SELECT id FROM clients WHERE user_id = :uid)
                """),
                {"pid": project_id, "uid": user_id},
            ).fetchone()
            is not None
        )

    if not has_access:
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=403, detail="You don't have access to this project"
        )


# =========================
# Endpoint: Get Comments
# =========================


@router.get(
    "/{project_id}/deliverables/{deliverable_id}/versions/{version}/comments",
    response_model=GetCommentsResponse,
    summary="Get version comments",
    description="Get all comments for a specific deliverable version, ordered by creation time.",
)
def get_version_comments(
    project_id: str,
    deliverable_id: str,
    version: str,
    current_user: Annotated[dict, Depends(get_current_user)],
    db: Annotated[Session, Depends(get_db)],
) -> GetCommentsResponse:
    role = current_user["role"]
    user_id = current_user["user_id"]

    _check_access(project_id, deliverable_id, user_id, role, db)

    rows = db.execute(
        text("""
            SELECT
                c.id, c.deliverable_id, c.deliverable_version,
                c.user_id, COALESCE(u.name, 'Unknown') AS user_name,
                c.content, c.created_at, c.parent_id
            FROM comments c
            LEFT JOIN users u ON c.user_id = u.id
            WHERE c.deliverable_id = :did AND c.deliverable_version = :ver
            ORDER BY c.created_at ASC
        """),
        {"did": deliverable_id, "ver": version},
    ).fetchall()

    comments = [
        CommentItem(
            id=r[0],
            deliverableId=r[1],
            deliverableVersion=r[2],
            userId=r[3],
            userName=r[4],
            content=r[5],
            createdAt=r[6].isoformat() if r[6] else "",
            parentId=r[7] or None,
        )
        for r in rows
    ]

    return GetCommentsResponse(comments=comments)


# =========================
# Endpoint: Add Comment
# =========================


@router.post(
    "/{project_id}/deliverables/{deliverable_id}/versions/{version}/comments",
    response_model=AddCommentResponse,
    status_code=201,
    summary="Add version comment",
    description="Add a comment to a specific deliverable version. Supports threaded replies via parentId.",
)
def add_version_comment(
    project_id: str,
    deliverable_id: str,
    version: str,
    body: AddCommentRequest,
    current_user: Annotated[dict, Depends(get_current_user)],
    db: Annotated[Session, Depends(get_db)],
) -> AddCommentResponse:
    role = current_user["role"]
    user_id = current_user["user_id"]

    _check_access(project_id, deliverable_id, user_id, role, db)

    # Validate parentId if provided
    if body.parentId:
        parent = db.execute(
            text("SELECT 1 FROM comments WHERE id = :pid"),
            {"pid": body.parentId},
        ).fetchone()
        if not parent:
            raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Parent comment not found")

    # =========================
    # Create Comment
    # =========================
    comment_id = _generate_id("cmt")
    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)

    db.execute(
        text("""
            INSERT INTO comments
                (id, deliverable_id, deliverable_version, user_id, content, created_at, parent_id)
            VALUES
                (:id, :did, :ver, :uid, :content, :created_at, :parent_id)
        """),
        {
            "id": comment_id,
            "did": deliverable_id,
            "ver": version,
            "uid": user_id,
            "content": body.content.strip(),
            "created_at": now,
            "parent_id": body.parentId,
        },
    )

    db.commit()

    user_name = db.execute(
        text("SELECT COALESCE(name, 'Unknown') FROM users WHERE id = :uid"),
        {"uid": user_id},
    ).fetchone()

    return AddCommentResponse(
        id=comment_id,
        deliverableId=deliverable_id,
        deliverableVersion=version,
        userId=user_id,
        userName=user_name[0] if user_name else "Unknown",
        content=body.content.strip(),
        createdAt=now.isoformat(),
        parentId=body.parentId,
    )

</final_file_content>

"""
Projects API router.

This module provides the router for project-related endpoints.
It aggregates all endpoints within the projects module.
"""

from fastapi import APIRouter
from app.api.projects.user_projects import router as user_projects_router
from app.api.projects.project_detail import router as project_detail_router
from app.api.projects.project_tasks import router as project_tasks_router
from app.api.projects.update import router as update_router
from app.api.projects.team_capacity import router as team_capacity_router
from app.api.projects.project_milestones import router as project_milestones_router
from app.api.projects.project_timesheets import router as project_timesheets_router

from app.api.projects.deliverables import router as deliverables_detail_router

router = APIRouter(prefix="/projects", tags=["projects"])

# Include all sub-routers
router.include_router(user_projects_router)
router.include_router(project_detail_router)

router.include_router(project_expenses_router)
router.include_router(project_budgets_router)
router.include_router(project_briefs_router)
router.include_router(project_create_deliverable_router)
router.include_router(deliverables_detail_router)

__all__ = ["router"]

<content>"use server";

/**
 * Server actions for deliverables.
 * Handles fetching and mutating deliverable data via the backend API.
 */

import { getAuthToken } from "@/lib/auth-cookies";

/**
 * Base URL for the backend API.
 */
const API_BASE_URL = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL || "http://localhost:8000";

// =========================
// Types
// =========================

export interface DeliverableVersion {
  id: string;
  deliverableId: string;
  version: string;
  uploadedBy: string;
  uploadedByName: string;
  uploadedAt: string;
  note: string;
  fileUrl: string;
  agencyApprovalStatus: string;
  agencyApprovedBy: string | null;
  agencyApprovedByName: string | null;
  agencyApprovedAt: string | null;
  clientApprovalStatus: string;
  clientApprovedBy: string | null;
  clientApprovedByName: string | null;
  clientApprovedAt: string | null;
}

export interface DeliverableDetail {
  id: string;
  projectId: string;
  milestoneId: string;
  milestoneTitle: string;
  taskId: string;
  taskTitle: string;
  title: string;
  description: string;
  assignedTo: string;
  assignedToName: string;
  status: string;
  currentVersion: string;
  approvedBy: string | null;
  approvedByName: string | null;
  approvedAt: string | null;
  versions: DeliverableVersion[];
}

// =========================
// Get Deliverable Detail
// =========================

export interface GetDeliverableDetailResult {
  success: boolean;
  error?: string;
  status?: number;
  deliverable?: DeliverableDetail;
}

export async function getDeliverableDetailAction(
  projectId: string,
  deliverableId: string
): Promise<GetDeliverableDetailResult> {
  const token = await getAuthToken();

  if (!token) {
    return { success: false, error: "Not authenticated", status: 401 };
  }

  try {
    const response = await fetch(
      `${API_BASE_URL}/api/projects/${projectId}/deliverables/${deliverableId}`,
      {
        method: "GET",
        headers: {
          "Content-Type": "application/json",
          Cookie: `access_token=${token}`,
        },
        cache: "no-store",
      }
    );

    if (!response.ok) {
      let errorMessage = "Failed to fetch deliverable";
      try {
        const errorData = await response.json();
        errorMessage = errorData.detail || errorMessage;
      } catch {
        // ignore
      }
      return { success: false, error: errorMessage, status: response.status };
    }

    const data = await response.json();
    return { success: true, deliverable: data };
  } catch (error) {
    console.error("Get deliverable detail error:", error);
    return { success: false, error: "An unexpected error occurred. Please try again." };
  }
}

// =========================
// Upload Version
// =========================

export interface UploadVersionData {
  version: string;
  note: string;
  fileUrl: string;
}

export interface UploadVersionResult {
  success: boolean;
  error?: string;
  status?: number;
  version?: DeliverableVersion;
}

export async function uploadDeliverableVersionAction(
  projectId: string,
  deliverableId: string,
  data: UploadVersionData
): Promise<UploadVersionResult> {
  const token = await getAuthToken();

  if (!token) {
    return { success: false, error: "Not authenticated", status: 401 };
  }

  try {
    const response = await fetch(
      `${API_BASE_URL}/api/projects/${projectId}/deliverables/${deliverableId}/versions`,
      {
        method: "POST",
        headers: {
          "Content-Type": "application/json",
          Cookie: `access_token=${token}`,
        },
        body: JSON.stringify(data),
        cache: "no-store",
      }
    );

    if (!response.ok) {
      let errorMessage = "Failed to upload version";
      try {
        const errorData = await response.json();
        errorMessage = errorData.detail || errorMessage;
      } catch {
        // ignore
      }
      return { success: false, error: errorMessage, status: response.status };
    }

    const version = await response.json();
    return { success: true, version };
  } catch (error) {
    console.error("Upload version error:", error);
    return { success: false, error: "An unexpected error occurred. Please try again." };
  }
}

// =========================
// Approve Agency
// =========================

export interface ApprovalResult {
  success: boolean;
  error?: string;
  status?: number;
  data?: {
    deliverableId: string;
    version: string;
    agencyApprovalStatus: string;
    clientApprovalStatus: string;
    currentVersion: string;
    status: string;
  };
}

export async function approveAgencyAction(
  projectId: string,
  deliverableId: string,
  version: string
): Promise<ApprovalResult> {
  const token = await getAuthToken();

  if (!token) {
    return { success: false, error: "Not authenticated", status: 401 };
  }

  try {
    const response = await fetch(
      `${API_BASE_URL}/api/projects/${projectId}/deliverables/${deliverableId}/versions/${version}/approve-agency`,
      {
        method: "POST",
        headers: {
          "Content-Type": "application/json",
          Cookie: `access_token=${token}`,
        },
        cache: "no-store",
      }
    );

    if (!response.ok) {
      let errorMessage = "Failed to approve (agency)";
      try {
        const errorData = await response.json();
        errorMessage = errorData.detail || errorMessage;
      } catch {
        // ignore
      }
      return { success: false, error: errorMessage, status: response.status };
    }

    const data = await response.json();
    return { success: true, data };
  } catch (error) {
    console.error("Approve agency error:", error);
    return { success: false, error: "An unexpected error occurred. Please try again." };
  }
}

// =========================
// Approve Client
// =========================

export async function approveClientAction(
  projectId: string,
  deliverableId: string,
  version: string
): Promise<ApprovalResult> {
  const token = await getAuthToken();

  if (!token) {
    return { success: false, error: "Not authenticated", status: 401 };
  }

  try {
    const response = await fetch(
      `${API_BASE_URL}/api/projects/${projectId}/deliverables/${deliverableId}/versions/${version}/approve-client`,
      {
        method: "POST",
        headers: {
          "Content-Type": "application/json",
          Cookie: `access_token=${token}`,
        },
        cache: "no-store",
      }
    );

    if (!response.ok) {
      let errorMessage = "Failed to approve (client)";
      try {
        const errorData = await response.json();
        errorMessage = errorData.detail || errorMessage;
      } catch {
        // ignore
      }
      return { success: false, error: errorMessage, status: response.status };
    }

    const data = await response.json();
    return { success: true, data };
  } catch (error) {
    console.error("Approve client error:", error);
    return { success: false, error: "An unexpected error occurred. Please try again." };
  }
}

// =========================
// Comments
// =========================

export interface CommentItem {
  id: string;
  deliverableId: string;
  deliverableVersion: string;
  userId: string;
  userName: string;
  content: string;
  createdAt: string;
  parentId: string | null;
}

export interface GetCommentsResult {
  success: boolean;
  error?: string;
  status?: number;
  comments?: CommentItem[];
}

export async function getDeliverableCommentsAction(
  projectId: string,
  deliverableId: string,
  version: string
): Promise<GetCommentsResult> {
  const token = await getAuthToken();

  if (!token) {
    return { success: false, error: "Not authenticated", status: 401 };
  }

  try {
    const response = await fetch(
      `${API_BASE_URL}/api/projects/${projectId}/deliverables/${deliverableId}/versions/${version}/comments`,
      {
        method: "GET",
        headers: {
          "Content-Type": "application/json",
          Cookie: `access_token=${token}`,
        },
        cache: "no-store",
      }
    );

    if (!response.ok) {
      let errorMessage = "Failed to fetch comments";
      try {
        const errorData = await response.json();
        errorMessage = errorData.detail || errorMessage;
      } catch {
        // ignore
      }
      return { success: false, error: errorMessage, status: response.status };
    }

    const data = await response.json();
    return { success: true, comments: data.comments };
  } catch (error) {
    console.error("Get comments error:", error);
    return { success: false, error: "An unexpected error occurred. Please try again." };
  }
}

export interface AddCommentData {
  content: string;
  parentId?: string;
}

export interface AddCommentResult {
  success: boolean;
  error?: string;
  status?: number;
  comment?: CommentItem;
}

export async function addDeliverableCommentAction(
  projectId: string,
  deliverableId: string,
  version: string,
  data: AddCommentData
): Promise<AddCommentResult> {
  const token = await getAuthToken();

  if (!token) {
    return { success: false, error: "Not authenticated", status: 401 };
  }

  try {
    const response = await fetch(
      `${API_BASE_URL}/api/projects/${projectId}/deliverables/${deliverableId}/versions/${version}/comments`,
      {
        method: "POST",
        headers: {
          "Content-Type": "application/json",
          Cookie: `access_token=${token}`,
        },
        body: JSON.stringify(data),
        cache: "no-store",
      }
    );

    if (!response.ok) {
      let errorMessage = "Failed to add comment";
      try {
        const errorData = await response.json();
        errorMessage = errorData.detail || errorMessage;
      } catch {
        // ignore
      }
      return { success: false, error: errorMessage, status: response.status };
    }

    const comment = await response.json();
    return { success: true, comment };
  } catch (error) {
    console.error("Add comment error:", error);
    return { success: false, error: "An unexpected error occurred. Please try again." };
  }
}
</content>

## Backend — New API Endpoints (6 files created)

Created `backend/app/api/projects/deliverables/` subfolder with:

1. **`detail.py`** — `GET /api/projects/{projectId}/deliverables/{deliverableId}`
   - Returns single deliverable with all versions, resolved user names, milestone title, task title
   - Full RBAC (agency_owner, ops_admin, project_manager, team_member, client)

2. **`versions.py`** — `POST /api/projects/{projectId}/deliverables/{deliverableId}/versions`
   - Uploads a new version with note and file URL
   - Resets approval statuses to pending
   - Updates deliverable status to in_review

3. **`approvals.py`** — Two endpoints:
   - `POST .../approve-agency` — Agency-side approval (agency_owner, project_manager)
   - `POST .../approve-client` — Client approval
   - When both approve, automatically sets version as current and status to approved

4. **`comments.py`** — Two endpoints:
   - `GET .../comments` — Fetch threaded comments with resolved user names
   - `POST .../comments` — Add comment with optional parentId for replies

5. **`__init__.py`** — Router aggregation for all deliverable sub-routers

6. **Modified `projects/__init__.py`** — Registered the new deliverables sub-router

## Frontend — Server Actions (1 file created)

Created `frontend/actions/deliverables.ts` with:
- `getDeliverableDetailAction` — Fetches deliverable detail
- `uploadDeliverableVersionAction` — Uploads new version
- `approveAgencyAction` — Agency approval
- `approveClientAction` — Client approval
- `getDeliverableCommentsAction` — Fetches version comments
- `addDeliverableCommentAction` — Adds/replies to comments
- All types defined locally (DeliverableVersion, DeliverableDetail, CommentItem, etc.)

## Frontend — Component Updated (1 file modified)

Updated `frontend/components/projects/deliverable-detail-page.tsx`:
- **Zero `@/data/*` imports** — all removed
- All types defined locally in the file (`DeliverableStatus`, etc.)
- Types from actions file used for API data (`DeliverableDetail`, `DeliverableVersion`, `CommentItem`)
- Proper async data fetching with `useCallback` + `useEffect`
- Loading/error states for initial fetch and comments
- Mutations (upload, approve, comment) refetch data after success
- No more `forceUpdate` hack — clean state management
